preamble: The shift from high front rounded [y] to mid-front unrounded [e] involves both lowering and unrounding.

examples.note: Common unrounding and lowering in several northern Germanic dialects.
Kentish dialectal variation showing unrounding to [e] (though often [y] > [i] elsewhere).

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
